author: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy
book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.
Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
It is Proved by the Testimonies of Scripture that God is Unchangeable. The Son of God Begotten, Not Made.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: It is Proved by the Testimonies of Scripture that God is Unchangeable. The Son of God Begotten, Not Made.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Those things which our faith holds and which reason in whatever way has traced out, are fortified by the testimonies of the divine Scriptures, so that those who by reason of feebler intellect are not able to comprehend these things, may believe the divine authority, and so may deserve to know.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: But let not those who understand, but are less instructed in ecclesiastical literature, suppose that we set forth these things from our own intellect rather than what are in those Books.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Accordingly, that God is unchangeable is written in the Psalms: "Thou shalt change them and they shall be changed; but Thou thyself art the same."

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: And in the book of Wisdom, concerning wisdom: "Remaining in herself, she renews all things."

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Whence also the Apostle Paul: "To the invisible, incorruptible, only God."

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: And the Apostle James: "Every best giving and every perfect gift is from above, descending from the Father of light, with whom there is no changeableness, neither obscuring of influence."

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Likewise because what He begat of Himself is what He Himself is, it is said in brief by the Son Himself: "I and the Father are one."

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: But because the Son was not made, since through Him were all things made, thus it is written: "In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and God was the Word; this was in the beginning with God.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: All things were made through Him, and without Him was made nothing;" that is, without Him was not anything made.
